headline: Matthew Perry Estate Auction Transforms Personal Memorabilia into Addiction Awareness Campaign
lede: The late actor's personal collection, including rare 'Friends' memorabilia, will hit the auction block next month, with proceeds supporting critical addiction recovery initiatives.
body:
Matthew Perry's legacy extends far beyond his iconic portrayal of Chandler Bing, with his upcoming estate auction promising to convert personal artifacts into a powerful advocacy platform for addiction recovery.

The comprehensive collection, curated from Perry's personal archives, will feature over two dozen original 'Friends' scripts, offering fans an unprecedented glimpse into the beloved sitcom's creative process. Notable items include scripts from fan-favorite episodes like "The One With Ross's Tan," which showcase the intricate behind-the-scenes work that defined the groundbreaking series.

Perry's non-profit foundation will leverage the auction's proceeds to continue his passionate mission of reducing addiction stigma and supporting recovery resources. This strategic approach transforms memorabilia into meaningful support, reflecting the actor's commitment to helping others battling substance abuse disorders.

Collectors and 'Friends' enthusiasts can expect a rare opportunity to own a piece of television history while simultaneously contributing to a critical social cause. The auction represents more than a commercial transaction — it's a testament to Perry's enduring impact beyond entertainment.
